sources_exist.note: Eleven cited pages returned accessible content, while inputs.pi_moat.sources[1] reached a generic Finbox page with no auditable metric content. Every canonical URL occurrence was nevertheless included below.

figures_match_sources.note: The deterministic dataset objects match exactly. The SEC exhibit supports a 5.0x-9.0x selected trading-comparable range and makes 7.5x plausible for an exit-tier reference, but it does not support the 4.5x small-firm buy multiple. Other material mismatches include a missing CCaaS-to-50%-by-2022 analog, no four-platform count, no cited pricing revenue shares, and a generic age statistic that does not measure owners aged 60+ in 561422.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Declared values are bounded and the low pi_dist/pi_moat direction is plausible for a melting, volume-billed service. However, the 60% frontline role weight is asserted as based on unspecified 6-digit staffing evidence, the 65% frontline automatable fraction cites uncited 41%/58% deflection figures, and t50=3 compresses an unsupported historical analog. These score-affecting judgments lack complete evidence.

rubric_consistent.note: Pricing deflation and the MELTING terminal mechanism follow the frozen rubric, but inputs.pi_moat.rationale explicitly uses Teleperformance and Concentrix valuation multiples to set C. The prompt restricts PE/M&A and deal-valuation evidence to B and M. The assigned ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is benign small stored-versus-recomputed drift on B, M, and S, but it does not cure the evidence or proxy violations.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and the terminal-value discussion candidly treats AI as product substitution. The no-proxy confirmation is inaccurate because V invokes unspecified staffing and deflection evidence not cited in the record, and the pricing cross-check states exact revenue shares and 2-3-year rebid cycles that its cited pages do not establish.

source_audits.note: The January 2025 Capstone page supports 41 broad BPO deals in 2024, 50% financial buyers, and a 41.2% year-over-year increase in add-ons. It does not identify or count four PE-backed platforms actively adding 561422 contact-center firms in the last 24 months.

The SEC exhibit is an August 2025 TELUS International valuation presentation and shows selected trading comparables at 5.0x-9.0x 2025E adjusted EBITDA. It contains no median paid multiple for $1M-$10M EBITDA private 561422 targets and does not support the uncited discount to 4.5x.

CMSWire reports AmplifAI's 25% operational-integration statistic for call centers generally. It does not isolate outsourced US telemarketing/contact-center bureaus in NAICS 561422, so it cannot establish that 25% of firms in this industry materially use AI.

The SEC-filed TELUS International valuation presentation directly shows a 5.0x-9.0x selected 2025E adjusted-EBITDA trading-comparable range, a 6.0x-10.0x precedent range, and a 6.0x-8.0x DCF terminal range. A 7.5x exit-tier estimate falls inside these large-platform reference ranges, though the broader 23.3x and 13x statements in the prose come from other uncited sources.

The article discusses CCaaS as a baseline and cites market revenue growth through 2029, but contains neither the quoted Gartner prediction of 50% adoption by 2022 nor evidence that availability began in 2012-2013. The claimed 8-10-year diffusion analog is unsupported.

The secondary page reports 37% of generic small-business owners are ages 55-73. It does not say more than half are over 55, does not permit extraction of a 60+ share, and has no 561422-specific owner population.

The vendor page describes per-minute, per-call, per-agent, and hybrid pricing and lists US onshore pricing at $20-$30 per hour. It gives no revenue-share mix, no 70-80% volume-billed share, and no 2-3-year RFP-cycle evidence supporting pi_dist=0.25.

The vendor article calls hourly pricing one of the most widely used models and estimates $29.40-$42 per US agent-hour in 2026. It does not quantify the claimed 70-80% volume-based revenue share or establish client ownership, rebid cycles, and capture sufficient to support pi_dist=0.25.

The live page reports Teleperformance EV/EBITDA of 3.85 as of 2026-07-20, not the cited 4.30. A trading multiple also does not directly establish the compliance, switching-cost, or AI-bypass mechanisms used to set pi_moat.
The URL reached a generic Finbox page but exposed no accessible Concentrix EV/EBITDA figure or supporting content. The cited 5.5x metric and the moat inference are therefore unauditable.

CMSWire supports broad contact-center AI adoption and operationalization statistics, but it does not establish pi_moat=0.25 or quantify the defensibility of compliance certifications, proprietary workflows, integrations, and direct client bypass for 561422 bureaus.

The page lists possible pricing models and $20-$30 US onshore hourly cost. It does not support the record's 50-60%, 20-25%, 10-15%, and 5-10% revenue shares, the quoted $29.40-$42 range, outcome premiums, or 20-35% blended-AI reductions.

reasons: inputs.current_adoption_pct.url cites a survey of contact centers generally, including in-house buyers outside NAICS 561422. Re-run with a source whose sample explicitly covers US outsourced contact-center operators, or label and bound the cross-industry extrapolation as an ESTIMATE before deriving inputs.t50_years.

inputs.historical_analogs.url does not contain the quoted 50%-by-2022 Gartner forecast or establish an 8-10-year CCaaS diffusion interval. Replace it with the original dated forecast plus a source for the starting penetration/date, then show a reproducible derivation for inputs.t50_years.

inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[0] asserts a 60% frontline wage share and 41%/58% deflection benchmarks without any HTTP citation in the record. Add accessible 561422-specific staffing evidence and the original deflection study with sample, metric definition, and population, then rebuild the role contributions.

inputs.active_consolidators.url reports broad BPO deal counts but not four active PE-backed 561422 platforms. Supply a named platform/add-on ledger covering the prior 24 months or mark the count as an evidence-bounded estimate.

inputs.buy_mult.url supplies large-public-company valuation ranges, not a median paid multiple for normalized private targets, while inputs.exit_mult.figure_quoted mixes uncited statistics into an otherwise relevant SEC range. Re-run with size-comparable transaction evidence and explicit EBITDA normalization for both inputs.

inputs.pricing_structure.url and inputs.pi_dist.sources do not establish the claimed revenue shares or contract-cycle assumptions. Add industry evidence that quantifies billing mix and rebid behavior; then reassess inputs.pi_dist and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation without unsupported percentages.

inputs.pi_moat.rationale uses Teleperformance and Concentrix valuation multiples to set C, contrary to the prompt rule confining PE/M&A and valuation evidence to B and M. Re-run pi_moat using only defensibility evidence such as switching costs, compliance gates, proprietary data, physical presence, and direct AI-bypass constraints.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S
